title: Migrate abduction-run dolt_mode server → embedded
description: Test the server→embedded conversion path. Steps planned: (1) bd export to refresh JSONL, (2) bd dolt stop, (3) backup .beads/dolt, (4) bd init --reinit-local --from-jsonl, (5) verify bead count + smoke commit. Backup the dolt dir first in case rollback needed.
notes:
EMBEDDED MIGRATION SUCCEEDED.

Steps applied:
1. bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl (refresh JSONL)
2. bd dolt stop (stop server)
3. mv .beads/dolt .beads/dolt.server-backup (preserve ~117M backup)
4. bd init --reinit-local --from-jsonl --prefix ar --non-interactive --destroy-token=DESTROY-ar
5. bd export + git add + git commit (smoke + close)

Results:
- dolt_mode: server → embedded
- project_id preserved: 21567b02-e2b4-45e5-965d-7de590efb8b2
- All 836 issues + 21 memories preserved
- New .beads/embeddeddolt is 43M (vs 117M server)
- Pre-commit hook intact (BEADS markers + timbers section)
- Smoke ar-l1p0 create+commit+close all worked

GOTCHAS DISCOVERED:
1. 'bd doctor' is not yet supported in embedded mode (1.0.3) — outputs error message with no checks
2. bd init --reinit-local creates an automatic git commit ('bd init: initialize beads issue tracking') that timbers' pre-commit hook flags as undocumented — must run timbers log to clear it
3. bd init --reinit-local also re-runs claude integration setup, which writes/refreshes CLAUDE.md beads section + .claude/settings.json (using BEGIN/END markers — preserves user content)
4. After reinit, .beads/dolt/ is recreated as a small placeholder (84K with .bd-dolt-ok marker + config.yaml) — embedded data lives in .beads/embeddeddolt/

Commits made on abduction-run main (5 ahead of origin):
- 232331f3 beads: close embedded smoke ar-l1p0
- d178b442 beads: migrate dolt_mode server → embedded
- 7ae67870 timbers: document
- d7fb0fae bd init: initialize beads (auto-commit from reinit)
- e7bb9717 beads: close ar-e3dl pilot smoke test
- 700a5773 beads: migrate hooks 0.63.3 → 1.0.3 + drop redundant export block

Cleanup pending: rm -rf .beads/dolt.server-backup (after confident)

title: Pilot 1.0.3 migration on abduction-run
description: End-to-end migration: chmod beads dir, switch to embedded if needed, install hooks (per Q1 decision), restore timbers hooks if displaced, smoke test create+commit+close, verify auto-export+auto-stage works without manual export block.
notes:
PILOT SUCCEEDED on abduction-run.

Steps applied (low-risk, in order):
1. chmod 700 .beads (silenced perm warning)
2. bd doctor --fix --yes (added embeddeddolt/ to .beads/.gitignore)
3. bd hooks install --force --beads (upgraded shims 0.63.3 → 1.0.3)
4. Manually deleted obsolete CUSTOM auto-export block from .beads/hooks/pre-commit (now redundant — export.auto + export.git-add defaults to true)
5. bd migrate --update-repo-id (cleared stale fingerprint mismatch)
6. bd vc commit (committed dolt schema migration)

Verification:
- bd doctor: 70 passed, 4 warnings (all pre-existing/benign), 0 errors
- Smoke bead ar-e3dl created+committed+closed across 2 git commits
- Pre-commit hook ran BEADS export AND timbers section both successfully
- Auto-export/auto-stage works (with 60s throttle — pre-commit forces export)
- core.hooksPath remains .beads/hooks (bd manages it)

LEFT ALONE (not part of pilot):
- dolt_mode = server (still server, not embedded — works fine, switching is optional)
- Pre-existing user uncommitted changes (.beads/issues.jsonl, devblog file)
- Bd Dolt remotes not configured (intentional per memory)

NOT YET PUSHED — awaiting user authorization (2 local commits ahead of origin/main).

Memory updates in abduction-run:
- Added: 1.0.3 hook pattern memory
- Removed: hooks-path-actually-beads-hooks-not-githooks (no longer relevant)
close_reason: Pilot + embedded migration both verified end-to-end on abduction-run

title: Verify bd hooks install behavior under 1.0.3
description: Determine whether bd hooks install --shared writes useful hooks under 1.0.3 (vs 0.63.3 .beads-hooks/ behavior), and whether .githooks/ umbrella is still needed for timbers + quality gates coexistence. Test on a scratch repo or abduction-run first.
notes:
VERIFIED 1.0.3 behavior on /tmp/bd-pilot scratch repo:

1. bd init defaults: embedded Dolt, .beads/hooks/ committed, core.hooksPath auto-set to .beads/hooks/, .claude/settings.json + CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md auto-installed
2. Auto-export+auto-stage on EVERY mutation — .beads/issues.jsonl appears as 'A' or 'M' in git status with no manual export call
3. bd hooks install --force preserves user content OUTSIDE BEGIN/END BEADS markers (verified)
4. timbers hooks install detects core.hooksPath and writes 'alongside beads hook' into .beads/hooks/pre-commit (and post-commit, post-rewrite)
5. Full lifecycle works: bd create → git status shows 'A .beads/issues.jsonl' → git commit ships it → bd close re-stages it
6. .beads/.gitignore handles all runtime files (dolt/, embeddeddolt/, locks, daemon files)

DECISION: Drop .githooks/ umbrella entirely. Adopt beads' blessed pattern: .beads/hooks/ committed, core.hooksPath managed by bd, user content (quality gates) added between BEADS and timbers sections.

DROP these workarounds:
- bd init --server (embedded works on Mac)
- Manual bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl in pre-commit
- Manual git add -f .beads/issues.jsonl in pre-commit
- Manual bd import in post-merge (auto-import handled)
- .githooks/ + core.hooksPath redirect (bd manages this)

title: beads 1.0.3 migration epic
description: Migrate active repos from beads 0.63.3 to 1.0.3, simplify hook patterns by adopting beads built-in auto-export/auto-import, drop --server workaround in favor of embedded Dolt. title: Migrate whitewater-journeys-ios to 1.0.3
description: iOS project — check for Xcode-specific hooks (SwiftLint). Apply pilot pattern, preserve any iOS-specific quality gates.
notes:
MIGRATION SUCCEEDED on whitewater-journeys-ios (main, 1 ephemeral CC worktree, NO git remote — local-only repo).

Steps:
1. chmod 700 .beads (perms 0750 → 0700)
2. bd doctor --fix --yes (gitignore)
3. bd hooks install --force --beads (1.0.3 shim, set core.hooksPath)
4. Manually fixed core.hooksPath to relative .beads/hooks (bd had set it to absolute path — would break worktrees)
5. git rm -r --cached .githooks/ + rm -f .git/hooks/* (removed legacy locations)
6. Smoke create wj-no0c
7. Commit hook migration (07542d7) — no quality gates in original .githooks/pre-commit so commit was fast
8. Embedded migration: bd export → bd dolt stop → mv .beads/dolt aside → bd init --reinit-local --discard-remote --from-jsonl --prefix wj
9. Smoke close + commit (d5aa313)

NEW GOTCHA: bd hooks install --force --beads can leave core.hooksPath as an ABSOLUTE path (saw in doctor output), which breaks worktrees that share repo config. Manual fix: git config core.hooksPath .beads/hooks (relative).

Stats preserved: 173 issues + 12 memories.

NOT DONE:
- No remote to push to (local-only repo) — migration is complete locally
- rm -rf .beads/dolt.server-backup-20260501 (~17M, gitignored)
close_reason: whitewater hook + embedded migration verified end-to-end (no remote, local-only repo)

title: Migrate redshifted to 1.0.3
description: Redshifted has no .githooks/ — never finished prior migration. Apply pilot pattern from scratch.
notes:
MIGRATION SUCCEEDED on redshifted (main, 2 worktrees: coordinator + economy-tune both 3-4 days idle).

Steps:
1. bd doctor --fix --yes (gitignore)
2. bd hooks install --force --beads (1.0.3 shims, set core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks)
3. Manually ported quality gates (lint-staged + just check) from .git/hooks/pre-commit into .beads/hooks/pre-commit between BEADS markers and timbers section
4. rm .git/hooks/{post-checkout,post-checkout.backup,post-commit,post-merge,post-merge.backup,pre-commit,pre-push,pre-push.bd-old,prepare-commit-msg} (legacy artifacts)
5. Smoke create+commit (just check ran cleanly: shader validation, typecheck, lint, tests)
6. Embedded migration: bd export → bd dolt stop → mv .beads/dolt aside → bd init --reinit-local --discard-remote --from-jsonl --prefix redshifted (DESTROY token required)
7. Smoke close + commit + push

NEW GOTCHA: bd init --reinit-local refused without --discard-remote because the repo had a Dolt remote configured (legacy). Adding --discard-remote --destroy-token=DESTROY-redshifted let it through. The destroy-remote action is fine because we don't use Dolt remotes — sync is via git+JSONL.

Stats preserved: 2557 issues + 52 memories. project_id e14fa2bf preserved. Embedded data ~287M → smaller after compaction.

2 commits pushed: 29c645eb (embedded migration) + 4cec3549/bcdefc73 (bd init auto-commit + timbers entry).

NOT done (left for user):
- rm -rf .beads/dolt.server-backup-20260501 (~287M, gitignored)
- Worktrees coordinator/economy-tune still have stale .beads/dolt-backup-* dirs from prior migrations (gitignored, no impact)
close_reason: redshifted hooks + embedded migration verified end-to-end and pushed

title: Test dolt migration
description: Verify dolt backend and remote push work. Close immediately after testing.

value: CC Agent tool has native isolation: "worktree" for ephemeral parallel subagent isolation — creates worktrees under .claude/worktrees/, auto-cleans up. Use bd worktree create only for persistent feature branches needing beads integration and merge guardrails.

timbers 0.19.0: default skip rules now auto-skip lockfiles, reverts of documented commits, .beads/issues.jsonl-only commits, and self-referential timbers entries. Per-repo .timbersignore at repo root extends rules. timbers status surfaces infrastructure-skipped count. No manual config needed — defaults handle the bulk.

1.0.3 server→embedded migration recipe: bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl; bd dolt stop; mv .beads/dolt .beads/dolt.server-backup; bd init --reinit-local --discard-remote --from-jsonl --prefix

--non-interactive --destroy-token=DESTROY-

. Required --discard-remote when a legacy Dolt remote exists (we use git+JSONL, not Dolt remotes).

bd doctor is a doorstop in embedded mode (1.0.3) — outputs an error message and runs no checks. Use bd ping (connectivity), bd hooks list (shim version), bd lint (convention drift), bd preflight (PR readiness), bd stale, bd orphans as alternatives.

beads 1.0.3 + timbers 0.19.0 hook pattern: bd init creates .beads/hooks/ (committed) and sets core.hooksPath; timbers hooks install detects this and writes alongside beads hook; user content outside BEGIN/END markers is preserved across reinstalls. Embedded Dolt is the default and works on macOS. export.auto + export.git-add are true by default — no manual export block needed.

eslint-plugin-sonarjs cognitive-complexity rule uses plain number arg in v4+: ['error', 15], NOT object form. Also sonarjs.configs.recommended may flag branded type aliases — disable sonarjs/redundant-type-aliases if using branded types.
WebFetch cannot access reddit.com or Medium paywalled articles. Use WebSearch to find alternative sources (mirrors, GitHub links) when those URLs come up.
